heading: Probation-wide launch of this service
whatsNew: Probation-wide launch of this service

Manage people on probation (MPOP) is now live for anyone working in the Probation Service with an NDelius username and password.

To support you with using this new service, we’re running webinars in every region during May and June including the National Security Division.

At the regional webinars you'll: 
listen to colleagues who have already started using the service  
watch demos 
find out how to get started 
find out what training and support is available 
have an opportunity to ask questions and have a go!  
Your Regional Change Lead will invite you to a webinar. You should prioritise attending it if you manage a caseload.
Add frequently used contacts in this service

You can now add the 10 contacts most frequently used by probation practitioners in this service, as well as appointments.

The contacts cover common communication contacts such as emails, texts, telephone calls and police liaisons.

We’ll be adding more contacts over the next few months.
Character limit increased for notes
To make it easier for you to add complete notes to a case, we’ve increased the character limit to 12,000.
Risk to Probation Staff flag

The new Risk to Probation Staff flag highlights when a person on probation poses a risk to probation staff.

This is a new registration type that is manually added and maintained within NDelius.
'Is this page useful?’ survey

We have added a ‘Is this page useful?’ survey at the bottom of every page (footer) to help you more readily indicate satisfaction or frustration with a particular page in the service.

If you click ‘yes’, you can add optional comments, then submit.

If you click ‘no’, you’re asked to give details and then submit.

This will help us to identify what’s working well and prioritise improvements.
Feedback banner

A ‘beta’ banner now appears at the top of every page to give you a quick and easy way to give us feedback. Click the link in the banner and an automatic email will open, addressed to our mpop-digital-team@justice.gov.uk inbox.
Calendar invitation bug fix
We’ve fixed a bug related to daylight savings, where the appointment time was correct on this service but displayed an hour early on the calendar invitation. Thanks to our users who reported this.

heading: Search and filter contacts more easily
whatsNew: Search and filter contacts more easily

To make it easier for you to see contacts on a case, there is now only one view of contacts. This view includes more contacts so that you quickly scan through what’s been happening on a case.

You can click on a contact name to read more about it without having to open the contact on a new page.

We’ve also added new categories to the filters to help you find previously added contacts, and you can toggle system-generated contacts on or off.
Tier explanation

To help you understand someone’s tier score, we’ve added a link in the header to an explanation of their tier calculation (for example, ‘B2’). This also explains tier history, protect and change factors.
New Probation Digital Services header

To make your experience of digital probation services more consistent, the title (known as the header) at the top of the Manage people on probation pages now says Probation Digital Services. Some other digital services already use this header.

heading: Reschedule appointments created on this service
whatsNew: Reschedule appointments

To help you keep your appointment schedule up to date, you can now reschedule appointments created on this service if they do not have outcomes. 
When you reschedule a future appointment, your calendar invite will automatically be updated with the new time and date. If the new date changes from the future to the past, you’ll get an email deleting the original calendar invitation.
Access to sentence plan

If a person on probation has a sentence plan using the new service, you can now access it via a link from the Risk page. This saves you from needing to sign in separately.

The Risk page has also been renamed to Risk and plan for people with sentence plans.

If they also have a new strength and needs assessment, you will not see their: 
OASys score history 
criminogenic needs, because they’re different from the ones in OASys
‘Risk to staff’ flag more visible
The ‘Risk to staff flag’ now shows during the appointment-booking process and on the appointment details page, as a more visual reminder of the person’s risk level.
Bug fixes and feedback

Thanks for continuing to share your feedback about the service. We’ve made some recent improvements based on what you told us:

The My cases page now displays a person on probation’s next contact with any part of the Probation Service. This resolves an issue where you could only see their next appointment if you were their probation practitioner.

The contacts count in the Activity and compliance section of the Overview page now states that any data is from all orders, including previous ones. Thanks for letting us know that this needed more clarity.

If you have outstanding issues to log, you can now access them from the appointment confirmation screen. This fixes a bug where the link was missing.

heading: Learn about online check ins for people on probation
whatsNew: About online check ins

Online check ins allow people on probation to submit a short video to check their identity, and answer 3 questions to tell practitioners how they are. This can be used to replace some face-to-face appointments where appropriate, or used alongside face-to-face appointments to help manage risk.
Help with using online check ins
You can get familiar with online check ins using our guidance (opens in new tab).
Bug fixes for Manage people on probation

To help you arrange appointments faster, we’ve improved page loading speed. Thanks to the users who reported this was slowing them down.

To make it easier for you to upload files, we’ve made some technical changes so that you can add one file at a time to an appointment outcome.

You can now arrange planned telephone contacts (NS) without needing to select a location. This fixes a bug that was causing errors.
Compliance page temporarily unavailable
The Compliance page is temporarily unavailable while we make some improvements. We’ll let you know as soon as it’s available again.

heading: View alerts and get calendar invitations for appointments
whatsNew: Get calendar invitations for appointments with people on probation

To make managing your appointment schedule easier, you now get an automatic meeting invitation in your inbox when you arrange an appointment with a person on probation.  The invitation appears as private in your calendar, so only you will see the appointment details which include: 
the title 
the time and date
a direct link to the appointment within the service  
If you arrange an appointment for someone else, they will get the private meeting invitation instead.
Type your appointment start and end times

To make it easier for you to add accurate appointment start and end times, you can now type the times you need, using the format hh:mm. 

Thanks to the users who said this would be quicker than selecting from a list.
Arrange past appointments

You can now arrange appointments in the past if they have an attended and complied outcome. 
Use NDelius if you need to arrange past appointments with other outcomes.

heading: View all outcomes to log on one page and other service improvements
whatsNew: The ‘You need to record an outcome for xx appointments’ link now goes to a page that lists every appointment without an outcome, so you can view them quicker.
Manage appointments in this service
You can use Manage people on probation (MPOP) to manage appointments arranged in this service, with a bug fix stopping those links from going to NDelius.
Thanks to our users at Harrow and Barnet PDU for this feedback. If you have any ideas or comments, we’d love to hear via mpop-digital-team@justice.gov.uk.
NDelius Risk flags
The risk flags now display the same data as on NDelius, correcting a bug where risk showed as ‘high’ instead of ‘medium’ for ‘risk to staff’.
Adding and viewing notes
To save you a click, you can now add notes right after selecting that a person on probation attended and complied. This change means you do not return to the appointment details before adding notes.
You can now read a long note in a dedicated page, thanks to a bug fix.
Clearer signposting between NDelius and MPOP

We’ve made it easier for you to see which service an appointment was created on. NDelius-created appointments now display ‘Manage on NDelius’ links on the ‘Appointments’ page, and on the ‘Activity log’ page for appointments without outcomes.
